samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data (single bit).</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        The module utilizes a 4-bit counter (cnt) to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received.
        Every eight din_serial inputs, dout_parallel will output.
        When all 8 serial data bits have been received, the valid output signal is set to 1,
        indicating that the parallel output data is valid. Otherwise, the valid output signal is set to 0,
        indicating that the parallel output data is not valid.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. Supports both signed and unsigned division operations. Outputs a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. Supports both signed and unsigned division operations. Outputs a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Data width conversion circuit that converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output. Generates valid_out and data_out signals based on the arrival of two 8-bit data inputs. Resets on low rst_n signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data to be converted.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data resulting from the width conversion.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>When valid_in is asserted, if flag is 0, store data_in in data_lock. If flag is 1, concatenate data_lock and data_in to generate data_out and assert valid_out. Reset all outputs on rst_n assertion.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="pe">
    <description>Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer. In the MAC_PE, there is a register that stores the partial sum (the intermediate accumulation result), and in each cycle, the result of “a multiplied by b” will be accumulated in this register, and the “c” shows the value of the register.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal. Defined as 1 for reset and 0 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="c" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output representing the accumulated result.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Parallel multiplier and accumulator using registers and an always block. Multiplies the input operands and accumulates the result into an output register. On reset, sets the register to 0. Otherwise, adds the product of the input operands to the register.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light system with red, yellow, and green indicators, controlled by a pedestrian button. The system operates based on a finite state machine with defined states for red, yellow, and green lights, adjusting durations based on the pedestrian request and the remaining green time.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Output signal representing the state of the red traffic light.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Output signal representing the state of the yellow traffic light.</output>
        <output id="green">Output signal representing the state of the green traffic light.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="idle">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="s1_red">State 1 - Red light.</state>
        <state id="s2_yellow">State 2 - Yellow light.</state>
        <state id="s3_green">State 3 - Green light.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="cnt" width="8" type="internal_counter">Internal counter for timing purposes.</register>
        <register id="state" width="2" type="current_state">Current state of the traffic light controller.</register>
        <register id="p_red" width="1" type="previous_signal">Previous value of the red signal.</register>
        <register id="p_yellow" width="1" type="previous_signal">Previous value of the yellow signal.</register>
        <register id="p_green" width="1" type="previous_signal">Previous value of the green signal.</register>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    idle: begin
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if (pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if (!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if (!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign clock = cnt;
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                red <= 0;
                yellow <= 0;
                green <= 0;
            end else begin
                red <= p_red;
                yellow <= p_yellow;
                green <= p_green;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_16bit">
    <description>Unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Multiplies two 16-bit unsigned numbers using shift-and-add method. Resets on rst_n low, updates on clk rising edge. Done flag indicates completion after 16 shifts.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel-to-serial converter, converting 4 parallel bits to 1 serial bit output (MSB first). Output valid_out indicates valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the next bit is outputted in the following 3 cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1. Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit. Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects the sequence '10011' in the input IN. Outputs MATCH=1 when the sequence is detected, and MATCH=0 otherwise. Supports continuous input and loops.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to be detected.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating whether the sequence has been matched.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always @(posedge CLK or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                MATCH <= 0;
            end else begin
                case ({IN, MATCH})
                    2'b10: MATCH <= 0;
                    2'b11: MATCH <= 1;
                    default: MATCH <= 0;
                endcase
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Frequency divider for generating 50MHz, 10MHz, and 1MHz clocks from a 100MHz input clock.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal (100MHz).</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal (50MHz).</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal (10MHz).</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal (1MHz).</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Counter for 50MHz
        reg [31:0] cnt_50;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_50 <= 0;
            end else begin
                cnt_50 <= cnt_50 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for 10MHz
        reg [31:0] cnt_10;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_10 <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (cnt_10 == 4) begin
                    cnt_10 <= 0;
                end else begin
                    cnt_10 <= cnt_10 + 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for 1MHz
        reg [31:0] cnt_1;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_1 <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (cnt_1 == 49) begin
                    cnt_1 <= 0;
                end else begin
                    cnt_1 <= cnt_1 + 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Generate 50MHz clock
        reg CLK_50_reg;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                CLK_50_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                CLK_50_reg <= ~CLK_50_reg;
            end
        end
        
        // Generate 10MHz clock
        reg CLK_10_reg;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                CLK_10_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (cnt_10 == 4) begin
                    CLK_10_reg <= ~CLK_10_reg;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Generate 1MHz clock
        reg CLK_1_reg;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                CLK_1_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (cnt_1 == 49) begin
                    CLK_1_reg <= ~CLK_1_reg;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign CLK_50 = CLK_50_reg;
        assign CLK_10 = CLK_10_reg;
        assign CLK_1 = CLK_1_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.

For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
        
        If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Edge detection module for detecting rising and falling edges of input signal a.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal to detect edges.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                rise <= 0;
                down <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (a && !prev_a) begin
                    rise <= 1;
                    down <= 0;
                end else if (!a && prev_a) begin
                    rise <= 0;
                    down <= 1;
                end else begin
                    rise <= 0;
                    down <= 0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_16bit">
    <description>Implement a 16-bit full adder using 8-bit adders in combinational logic.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit input operand B.</input>
        <input id="Cin">Carry-in input.</input>
        <output id="y" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="Co">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Instantiate two 8-bit adders for the upper and lower halves
        wire [7:0] sum_upper;
        wire carry_out_upper;
        wire [7:0] sum_lower;
        wire carry_out_lower;
        
        // First 8-bit adder
        assign {carry_out_upper, sum_upper} = a[15:8] + b[15:8] + Cin;
        
        // Second 8-bit adder
        assign {carry_out_lower, sum_lower} = a[7:0] + b[7:0] + carry_out_upper;
        
        // Combine results
        assign y[15:8] = sum_upper;
        assign y[7:0] = sum_lower;
        assign Co = carry_out_lower;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers to store intermediate values and control the multiplication process.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="size" value="4">Data width for inputs and outputs.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        Extension of input signals:
        The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
        
        Multiplication operation:
        The module uses a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.
        For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
        If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
        If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0').
        
        Add of partial products:
        The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.
        On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
        
        Final product calculation:
        On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal used for synchronous write operations.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal used for synchronous read operations.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal. Active low, defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal. Triggered to initiate write operations.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal. Triggered to initiate read operations.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input. Configurable width representing the data to be written into the FIFO.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal. Indicates if the FIFO is full and cannot accept further write operations.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal. Indicates if the FIFO is empty and cannot provide any data for read operations.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output. Configurable width representing the data read from the FIFO.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="8">Configurable width of the FIFO data input/output.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="16">Configurable depth of the FIFO memory.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instantiation
        <instance id="ram_instance" module_id="dual_port_RAM">
            <port_map>
                <input port_id="wclk" source_id="wclk"/>
                <input port_id="wenc" source_id="wen"/>
                <input port_id="waddr" source_id="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
                <input port_id="wdata" source_id="wdata"/>
                <input port_id="rclk" source_id="rclk"/>
                <input port_id="renc" source_id="ren"/>
                <input port_id="raddr" source_id="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
                <output port_id="rdata" destination_id="rdata"/>
            </port_map>
        </instance>
        
        // Write pointer management
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width="[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]">Binary representation of the write address pointer.</reg>
        <always_combination>
            if (winc && wrstn) begin
                waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
            end else if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr_bin <= 0;
            end
        </always_combination>
        
        // Read pointer management
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width="[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]">Binary representation of the read address pointer.</reg>
        <always_combination>
            if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
            end else if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr_bin <= 0;
            end
        </always_combination>
        
        // Gray code conversion for write pointer
        <wire id="wptr" width="[$clog2(DEPTH)]">Gray code representation of the write pointer.</wire>
        <assign wptr[0] = waddr_bin[0]; // LSB remains unchanged
        <assign wptr[1] = waddr_bin[1] ^ waddr_bin[0];
        <assign wptr[2] = waddr_bin[2] ^ waddr_bin[1];
        <assign wptr[3] = waddr_bin[3] ^ waddr_bin[2];
        
        // Gray code conversion for read pointer
        <wire id="rptr" width="[$clog2(DEPTH)]">Gray code representation of the read pointer.</wire>
        <assign rptr[0] = raddr_bin[0]; // LSB remains unchanged
        <assign rptr[1] = raddr_bin[1] ^ raddr_bin[0];
        <assign rptr[2] = raddr_bin[2] ^ raddr_bin[1];
        <assign rptr[3] = raddr_bin[3] ^ raddr_bin[2];
        
        // Buffer registers for synchronized pointers
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width="[$clog2(DEPTH)]">Buffer register for write pointer synchronization.</reg>
        <always_combination>
            if (winc && wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end else if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end
        </always_combination>
        
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width="[$clog2(DEPTH)]">Buffer register for read pointer synchronization.</reg>
        <always_combination>
            if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end else if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end
        </always_combination>
        
        // Full/empty signal generation
        <wire id="wfull">Write full signal indicator.</wire>
        <assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[2:0]});
        
        <wire id="rempty">Read empty signal indicator.</wire>
        <assign rempty = (rptr == wptr);
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Asynchronously manages data flow between a producer and consumer using a dual-port RAM structure. Implements mechanisms for writing and reading data while handling full and empty conditions through Gray code conversion and pointer buffering.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_32bit">
    <description>Implement a carry-lookahead 32-bit adder using the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="S" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="C32">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Instantiate two 16-bit CLA blocks for the first and second halves of the 32-bit inputs
        wire [15:1] S1;
        wire C16;
        cla_block_16bit cla1 (.A(A[16:1]), .B(B[16:1]), .Cin(1'b0), .Sum(S1), .Cout(C16));
        
        wire [15:1] S2;
        wire C32;
        cla_block_16bit cla2 (.A(A[32:17]), .B(B[32:17]), .Cin(C16), .Sum(S2), .Cout(C32));
        
        // Concatenate the outputs of the two CLA blocks to form the final sum
        assign S = {S2, S1};
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit ripple carry adder with pipelining stages and registers for synchronization.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation.</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal indicating the availability of the result.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Pipeline stages
        reg [63:0] adda_reg;
        reg [63:0] addb_reg;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage1;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage2;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage3;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage4;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage5;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage6;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage7;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage8;
        
        // Registers for enabling pipeline stages
        reg i_en_reg;
        reg i_en_reg1;
        reg i_en_reg2;
        reg i_en_reg3;
        reg i_en_reg4;
        reg i_en_reg5;
        reg i_en_reg6;
        reg i_en_reg7;
        reg i_en_reg8;
        
        // Register for output enable signal
        reg o_en_reg;
        
        // Assigning inputs to registers
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                adda_reg <= 64'd0;
                addb_reg <= 64'd0;
                i_en_reg <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg1 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg2 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg3 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg4 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg5 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg6 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg7 <= 1'b0;
                i_en_reg8 <= 1'b0;
                o_en_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                adda_reg <= adda;
                addb_reg <= addb;
                i_en_reg <= i_en;
                i_en_reg1 <= i_en_reg;
                i_en_reg2 <= i_en_reg1;
                i_en_reg3 <= i_en_reg2;
                i_en_reg4 <= i_en_reg3;
                i_en_reg5 <= i_en_reg4;
                i_en_reg6 <= i_en_reg5;
                i_en_reg7 <= i_en_reg6;
                i_en_reg8 <= i_en_reg7;
                
                // Calculate sums for each pipeline stage
                sum_stage1 <= adda_reg + addb_reg;
                sum_stage2 <= sum_stage1 + {63'd0, i_en_reg};
                sum_stage3 <= sum_stage2 + {63'd0, i_en_reg1};
                sum_stage4 <= sum_stage3 + {63'd0, i_en_reg2};
                sum_stage5 <= sum_stage4 + {63'd0, i_en_reg3};
                sum_stage6 <= sum_stage5 + {63'd0, i_en_reg4};
                sum_stage7 <= sum_stage6 + {63'd0, i_en_reg5};
                sum_stage8 <= sum_stage7 + {63'd0, i_en_reg6};
                
                // Update output enable signal
                o_en_reg <= i_en_reg8;
            end
        end
        
        // Assigning outputs
        assign result = {sum_stage8[63], sum_stage8};
        assign o_en = o_en_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module. Detects rising edges of data_in and generates a pulse output data_out when a complete pulse is detected (from 0 to 1 to 0 over three cycles).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always block sensitive to the positive edge of clk and the negative edge of rst_n.
        In the reset condition (~rst_n), initialize the state register to the first state and set data_out to 0.
        For each state, check the value of data_in and transition to the next state accordingly.
        If the current state indicates the end of a pulse, set data_out to 1; otherwise, set it to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>

There is no output when there are fewer than four data_in inputs in the interim. Along with the output data_out, a cycle of valid_out=1 will appear as a signal. The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.</implementation>

<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter with specific state transitions.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        On every rising edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the counter value is updated.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the counter value (Q) is set to 0.
        Otherwise, if the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 0, the counter increments by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 1 at the most significant bit position.
        If the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 1, the counter decrements by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 0 at the most significant bit position.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="RAM">
    <description>Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal to initiate a write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal to initiate a read operation.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the read operation.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output signal representing the data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="6">Bit width of the RAM entries.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="8">Depth of the RAM (number of entries).</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="RAM" width_description="[DEPTH-1:0][WIDTH-1:0]" initial_value="000000">Memory array for storing data.</reg>
        <reg id="read_data" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]" initial_value="000000">Register to hold the read data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Write operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset all memory locations to 0
                integer i;
                for (i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                    RAM[i] <= 6'b000000;
                end
            end else if (write_en) begin
                // Write data to the specified address
                RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
            end
        end
        
        // Read operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset read data to 0
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end else if (read_en) begin
                // Read data from the specified address
                read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
            end else begin
                // Clear read data if read_en is not active
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="right_shifter">
    <description>Right shifter that takes an 8-bit input and outputs the shifted result after each clock cycle.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="d">Input signal to be right-shifted.</input>
        <output id="q" width_description="[7:0]">Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Initializes q to 0. Shifts q right by 1 bit on each rising edge of clk. Inserts d into the most significant position of q.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>A multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer that captures data_in when data_en is high, ensuring synchronization between two different clocks (clk_a and clk_b).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal of size 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal that controls the selection operation.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal of size 4 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Data Register
        reg [3:0] data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                data_reg <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                data_reg <= data_in;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Data Register
        reg en_data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                en_data_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_data_reg <= data_en;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Control Registers
        reg en_clap_one, en_clap_two;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                en_clap_one <= 1'b0;
                en_clap_two <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
                en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
            end
        end
        
        // Output Assignment
        reg [3:0] dataout;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                dataout <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                if (en_clap_two) begin
                    dataout <= data_reg;
                end else begin
                    dataout <= dataout;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
